provenance.source: NASA Earth Fact Sheet (mean volumetric radius)
provenance.note: radius is the mean; Earth is an oblate spheroid (equatorial 6378.1 km, polar 6356.8 km) — sphere rendering is a knowing simplification

orbit._angles_note: Omega and omega are J2000 mean values and PRECESS (nodal ~18.6 yr retrograde, apsidal ~8.85 yr prograde) — held fixed here, see render_approximations

render_approximations.what: Keplerian two-body ellipse with FIXED mean elements — no apsidal/nodal precession, no perturbations (evection, variation)
render_approximations.why: rung 2 renders the true ellipse shape and tilt (e, i now in the geometry, not just the data); the remaining gap is that the real Moon is heavily perturbed by the Sun and its ellipse rotates
render_approximations.correction_path: JPL Horizons ephemerides (rung 3+) — real positions, not elements
render_approximations.expected_error: up to ~5,000 km positional vs true ephemeris (lunar perturbation terms) — labeled, not hidden

retired.what: orbit drawn circular at semi-major axis, in the ecliptic plane

provenance.note: random points for depth perception — NOT real star positions. Correction path: Hipparcos/Gaia bright-star catalog.
fixed directional light standing in for the Sun; no Sun object exists yet (rung 3)
